metadata.summary: NABS: Not a beamline scientist. Beamline automatation that should be handled by code, not by people.

documentation:
# nabs
Not a Beamline Scientist

This is the landing site for shared code that interfaces with bluesky and
pcdsdaq for beamline automation and data collection routines for LCLS photon
science.

It is named NABS, Not a Beamline Scientist, because it is the go-to when the
question is "Who should be doing this procedure?" and the answer is "Not a
Beamline Scientist!", e.g. it is something that should not be done by hand.

functions.name: nabs.visualizations.show_shot_targets
documentation:
Display a plot with targets.

This class is used in conjunction witht the `XYGridStage` object from
`pcdsdevices` as well as the `fixed_target_scan` from `nabs.plans`.
It displays a scatter plot with the targets that have been scanned (& shot)
with `fixed_target_scan`, and the targets that are still available.
It uses the information saved in an yaml file for a specific sample
to get the x and y positions as well as the last target that has been shot.
This information is saved with the help of the `XYGridStage` object.

Parameters
----------
sample_name : str
    The name of the sample file to plot the graph for.
path : str
    The path of the sample file.
mn_format : bool
    Indicates if the graph should be represented in terms of M and N
    points rather than x and y positions.

modules.name: nabs.streams
documentation: Standard data streams for automated routines

classes.name: nabs.streams.AverageStream

methods.name: nabs.streams.AverageStream.start
documentation:
Create the stream after seeing the start document

The callback looks for the 'average' key in the start document to
configure itself.

methods.name: nabs.streams.AverageStream.event
documentation: Send an Event through the stream

methods.name: nabs.streams.AverageStream.stop
documentation: Delete the stream when run stops

Stream that averages data points together

As event documents are emitted from the `bluesky.run_engine.RunEngine`
they are collected by `AverageStream`, averaged, and re-emitted
as a secondary event stream. This
allows callbacks to subscribe to the average stream for more elegant
visualization and analysis.

The average stream can either be constructed with a specific number of
points to average or this information can be passed through in the start
document metadata. See the {meth}`.start` for more information.

# Parameters

num

: Number of points to average together

# Attributes

last_event

: Data from the last event emitted by the stream. Used for limited access
  to processed data without the need for a subscription.

functions.name: nabs.utils.add_named_kwargs_to_signature
documentation:
Add named keyword arguments with default values to a function signature.

Parameters
----------
func_or_signature : inspect.Signature or callable
    The function or signature.

kwargs : dict
    The dictionary of kwarg_name to default_value.

Returns
-------
modified_signature : inspect.Signature
    The modified signature with additional keyword arguments.

functions.name: nabs.utils.orange
documentation:
Get scan points based on the type of `num`.  If `num` is an
integer, interpret as the number of points in a scan.  If `num`
is a float, interpret it as a step size.

Modified to include end points.

Parameters
----------
start : int or float
    The first point in the scan

end : int or float
    The last point in the scan

num : int or float
    if int, the number of points in the scan.
    if float, step size

Returns
-------
list
    a list of scan points

classes.name: nabs.utils.InvertedSignal

methods.name: nabs.utils.InvertedSignal.forward
documentation: Invert the value

methods.name: nabs.utils.InvertedSignal.inverse

Invert another `ophyd` Signal

Parameters

derived_from: `ophyd.signal.Signal`
    Ophyd Signal

classes.name: nabs.utils.ErrorSignal

methods.name: nabs.utils.ErrorSignal.forward
documentation: Invert the value

methods.name: nabs.utils.ErrorSignal.inverse
documentation: Invert the value

Signal that reports the absolute error from a provided target

Parameters

derived_from : `ophyd.signal.Signal`

target : float
    Position of zero error

classes.name: nabs.utils.Process
documentation:
A subclass of multiprocessing.Process that makes exceptions
accessible by the parent process.

modules.name: nabs.callbacks
documentation:
Callbacks for subscription to the Bluesky `RunEngine`

This is the LCLS counterpart to `bluesky.callbacks`

Callbacks in this module expect and operate on the `start`, `event`,
`stop`, etc documents.

classes.name: nabs.callbacks.ELogPoster

methods.name: nabs.callbacks.ELogPoster.start
documentation: Post plan information on start document

methods.name: nabs.callbacks.ELogPoster.descriptor
documentation: Initialize table information

methods.name: nabs.callbacks.ELogPoster.stop
documentation: Post to ELog on plan close (stop document)

Callback to post messages to the elog, primarily related to plan setup and
results. This callback relies on and must be instantiated after the
BestEffortCallback and HutchELog.

To subscribe:

```python
elogc = ELogPoster(elog, IPython.get_ipython())
elogc_uid = RE.subscribe(elogc)
```

To enable posting for a specific run:

```python
RE(plan(), post=True)
```

# Parameters

bec

: Instance of BestEffortCallback subscribed to the current RunEngine

elog

: Instance of HutchELog connected to the desired logbook

classes.name: nabs.callbacks.BECOptionsPerRun

methods.name: nabs.callbacks.BECOptionsPerRun.restore_settings
documentation: Reset bec settings if they exist.

Callback that reads options from metadata to decide whether or not
to plot/print table.

Settings should only persist for one run, after which the settings
are reverted. Options can be set by passing metadata to the RE:

% code-block: python
%
% # Can pass as metadata in the plan
% RE(bp.scan([], motor, -1, 1, 5, md={'disable_plots': True}))
%
% # Or as kwargs to the RunEngine
% RE(plan(), disable_table=False)

modules.name: nabs.preprocessors
documentation:
Wrappers and decorators to modify existing plans.

This is the LCLS counterpart to `bluesky.preprocessors`.

This module contains "wrapper" functions that take a plan as an argument
and yield messages from a new, modified plan, as well as "decorator"
functions that can be applied to `bluesky` plan functions to return new
plan functions with modifications.

functions.name: nabs.preprocessors._get_daq
documentation:
Helper function to get the active DAQ object.

This also wraps the `pcdsdaq` import because `pcdsdaq` is an optional
dependency of `nabs`. This will fail unless `pcdsdaq` is installed.

# Returns

daq

: The DAQ (data aquisition) system `bluesky`-compatible control object.

functions.name: nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_wrapper
documentation:
Wrapper to turn an open plan into a standard LCLS DAQ step plan.

This inserts the DAQ object into every `bluesky.plan_stubs.trigger` and
`bluesky.plan_stubs.read` pair, ensuring events are taken at every
bundle. It also stages the `pcdsdaq.daq.Daq` and yields an appropriate
`bluesky.plan_stubs.configure` message using the input arguments
and all motors moved prior to the first data point.

The DAQ trigger and the DAQ read always go first, before any other triggers
or reads, to ensure all events are recorded.

If the DAQ is manually passed into the wrapped plan, and it is the first
detector in the list, we will skip adding a redundant trigger/read. If the
DAQ is manually passed in as the second detector or later we will end up
with two triggers and two reads, which can cause problems. Running a scan
like this will raise a `TypeError`.

See `daq_step_scan_decorator` for the function decorator version.

# Parameters

plan

: A bluesky plan that yields bluesky Msg objects.

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

# Returns

daq_step_plan

: The same plan as before, but modified appropriately to the run the DAQ
  at every step. This will be an open generator.

functions.name: nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator
documentation:
Decorator to turn a plan function into a standard LCLS DAQ step plan.

This adds the standard DAQ configuration arguments
events, duration, record, and use_l3t onto the plan function
and wraps the plan in the `daq_step_scan_wrapper` to properly
execute a step scan.

See `daq_step_scan_standard_args` for argument specifications for the
standard DAQ configuration arguments.

Parameters
----------
plan : plan
    A bluesky plan that yields bluesky Msg objects.

Returns
-------
daq_step_plan : plan
    The same plan as before, but modified appropriately for DAQ use.
    This will be a callable generator function.

functions.name: nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_standard_args
documentation:
No-op function to hold template parameter info for generated docs.

Parameters
----------
events : int, optional
    Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
    duration argument or the last configured value.

duration : int or float, optional
    Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
    argument or the last configured value.

record : bool, optional
    Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
    we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t : bool, optional
    Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
    to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

functions.name: nabs.preprocessors.daq_during_wrapper
documentation:
Wrap a plan so that the DAQ runs at the same time.

This can be used with an ordinary `bluesky` plan that you'd like the daq
to run along with. This also stages the DAQ so that the run start/stop
will be synchronized with the bluesky runs.

Note that this is not a calib cycle scan. See
`daq_step_scan_wrapper` and `daq_step_scan_decorator`
for the calib cycle variant.

All configuration must be done by supplying config kwargs to this wrapper.

This must be applied outside the run_wrapper.

The `daq_during_decorator` is the same as the
`daq_during_wrapper`, but it is meant to be used as a function
decorator.

Internally, this uses the flyer interface of the `pcdsdaq.daq.Daq`
object.

# Parameters

plan

: A bluesky plan that yields bluesky Msg objects.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

controls

: If provided, values from these will make it into the DAQ data
  stream as variables. For this purpose, the `.position` and
  `.value` attributes will be checked, followed by the `.get()`
  method.

# Returns

daq_during_plan

: The same plan as before, but modified appropriately to run the DAQ at
  the same time.

functions.name: nabs.preprocessors.step_size_decorator
documentation:
Grab the last argument (number of steps), and intepret as
- step size if float
- number of steps if integer

Only works on step scans in one dimension.

Parameters
----------
plan : plan
    A bluesky plan that yields bluesky Msg objects.  Must be
    a scan in one dimension, with the last argument being
    the number of scan points / step size.

Returns
-------
step_size_plan : plan
    The same plan as before, but modified appropriately to
    differentiate between step size and number of steps.
    This will be a callable generator function.

classes.name: nabs.preprocessors._Dummy
documentation:
Class to sub in for the DAQ when we need to drop a message.

You can't just remove a message entirely with
`bluesky.preprocessors.plan_mutator`, you need
to yield a compatible message. To accomplish this we sub in a dummy object
for the daq to create a no-op with the right return value.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_streams.stepscan
documentation: Old example scan from bluesky deprecated submodule.

classes.name: nabs.exceptions.NabsError
documentation: Base class for nabs-related errors.

classes.name: nabs.exceptions.DaqNotConfiguredError
documentation: The DAQ has not yet been configured.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_daq_step_scan_args
documentation:
Basic args and message inspection tests.

Can I decorate a scan?
Can I call a decorated scan at all?
Does a decorated scan produce the messages I expect?

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_daq_step_scan_run_events
documentation: Actually run a scan and make sure it doesn't error out.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_daq_step_scan_run_duration
documentation: Again, but with duration arg instead of events arg

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_daq_step_scan_preconfigured
documentation: Again, but the user passes neither! (configured first)

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_daq_step_scan_misconfigured
documentation: Check that we raise a TypeError when DAQ is passed as second detector.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_daq_during_decorator
documentation: Run a daq during scan and make sure the daq is running during it.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_preprocessors.test_noop_coverage
documentation: Call the documentation function to cover the line.

modules.name: nabs.simulators
documentation: Simulation and validation functions for plans

functions.name: nabs.simulators.patch_sys_modules
documentation:
takes a list of module names as strings and stores them,
replaces them with a raiser, and replaces them after

Need to use exec/eval here due to pass-by-reference issues

functions.name: nabs.simulators.check_open_close
documentation:
Check if a plan is open and closed correctly.

Nested plans are allowed if they are labeled properly
with run tags.

Raises if a violation is found
If a message has as run key, all messages in that run must also.

Parameters
----------
plan : iterable or generator
    Must yield `Msg` objects

Raises
------
ValidError
    If plan is not constructed correctly

functions.name: nabs.simulators.check_stray_calls
documentation:
Validate that plan does not invoke any caput functionality
outside of messages.

Runs the check within a multiprocessing.Process to isolate
namespace from the parent process.

Relies on the pre-existing knowledge of which methods make calls
to pyepics/caput functionality.

This does not work on Windows.

Parameters
----------
plan : iterable or generator
    Must yield `Msg` objects

Raises
------
ValidError
    If attempts to access any forbidden methods

functions.name: nabs.simulators.validate_plan
documentation:
Validate plan with all available checkers.

# Parameters

plan: generator function

: Once called, must yield `Msg` objects.

validators: list of check functions

: functions to run on the provided plan. These should take an
  evaluated plan (generator) as input, and raise exceptions on
  failure. (should NOT take generator functions)

# Returns

boolean

: Indicates if validation was successful (`True`) or failed
  (`False`).

str

: Error message that explains the reason for validation
  failure. Empty string if validation is successful.

functions.name: nabs.simulators.summarize_plan
documentation:
Print summary of plan

Prints a minimal version of the plan, showing only moves and
where events are created.

Taken from bluesky.simulators.summarize_plan, but adapted to
print lcls-daq information specifically

Parameters
----------
plan : iterable
    Must yield `Msg` objects

classes.name: nabs.version.VersionProxy
documentation:
Version handling helper that pairs with setuptools-scm.

This allows for pkg.__version__ to be dynamically retrieved on request by
way of setuptools-scm.

This deferred evaluation of the version until it is checked saves time on
package import.

This supports the following scenarios:

1. A git checkout (.git exists)
2. A git archive / a tarball release from GitHub that includes version
    information in .git_archival.txt.
3. An existing _version.py generated by setuptools_scm
4. A fallback in case none of the above match - resulting in a version of
    0.0.unknown

modules.name: nabs._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_plans.test_duration_scan
documentation: Run the duration scan and check the messages it creates.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_plans.test_delay_scan
documentation: Check the delay scan, verify that velo is set appropriately.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_plans.test_daq_delay_scan
documentation: Check that daq_delay_scan's arguments all work.

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_plans.assert_scan_has_daq
documentation:
Go through a plan's messages and verify it runs the daq correctly.

This is intended to be applied to any "standard" plan that is just a
built-in bluesky plan with the daq added to it.

We check for the following things:
1. The DAQ is staged (exactly once)
2. The DAQ is configured (exactly once)
3. The DAQ is triggered
4. The DAQ is read
5. The DAQ is unstaged (exactly once)

functions.name: nabs.tests.test_plans.daq_test
documentation: Check the messages for daq and try to run the plan with RE.

modules.name: nabs.plan_stubs
documentation:
Plan pieces that may be useful for assembling plans.

This is the LCLS counterpart to `bluesky.plan_stubs`.

The plans in this module are not meant to be run individually, instead these
are intended as building blocks for other complete plans.

functions.name: nabs.plan_stubs.measure_average
documentation:
Measure an average over a number of shots from a set of detectors.

# Parameters

detectors

: List of detectors to read

num

: Number of shots to average together

delay

: Time delay between successive readings. See `bluesky.plans.count`
  for more details

stream

: If a plan will call `measure_average` multiple times, a single
  `AverageStream` instance can be created and then passed in on each
  call. This allows other callbacks to subscribe to the averaged data
  stream. If no `AverageStream` is provided then one is created for the
  purpose of this function.

# Returns

averaged_event

: A dictionary of all the measurements taken from the list of detectors
  averaged for `num` shots. The keys follow the same naming convention
  as that will appear in the event documents i.e "\{name}\_\{field}"

# Notes

The returned average dictionary will only contain keys for 'number' or
'array' fields. Field types that can not be averaged such as 'string' will
be ignored, do not expect them in the output.

functions.name: nabs.plan_stubs.update_sample
documentation:
Update the current sample information after a run.

Updates the `status` values of each target in the sample,
from `False` to `True` to indicate that it is shot.

# Parameters

sample_name

: A name to identify the sample grid, should be snake_case style.

path

: Path to the `.yml` file. Defaults to the path defined when
  creating this object.

n_shots

: Indicates how many targets have been shot.

functions.name: nabs.plan_stubs.get_sample_targets
documentation:
Get the `xx` and `yy` target information from a saved sample.

Given a sample name, get the x, y grid points that are mapped for that
sample.

# Parameters

sample_name

: The name of the sample to get the mapped points from. To see the
  available mapped samples call the `mapped_samples` method.

path

: Path to the samples yaml file.

# Returns

`xx`, `yy`

: Returns two lists of dictionaries, with information about the targets.

modules.name: nabs.optimize
documentation: Support for generic optimization routines through `bluesky`.

functions.name: nabs.optimize.minimize
documentation: Minimize the value of an `ophyd.signal.Signal`.

functions.name: nabs.optimize.maximize
documentation: Maximize the value of an `ophyd.signal.Signal`.

functions.name: nabs.optimize.walk_to_target
documentation:
Walk the motor until a signal reaches our target

Similar to the `maximize` and `minimize`. There are options
for multiple algorithms to dictate the scanning procedure. This may change
the interpretation of values passed into this scanning procedure.

Parameters
----------
signal : `ophyd.signal.Signal`
    Signal to maximize

motor : `ophyd.ophydobj.OphydObject`
    Any set-able object

tolerance : float, optional
    The tolerance in which our motor is

average : int, optional
    Choice to take an average of points at each point in the scan

limits : tuple, optional
    Limit the region the scan will search within. If this is not provided,
    the soft limits of the signal will be used. In this case, these must be
    configured or the scan will not be allowed to continue.

method : str, optional
    Choice of optimization methods

md : dict, optional
    metadata

functions.name: nabs.optimize.optimize
documentation:
Generic optimization method

This method serves as the switchyard for various methods and requirements
necessary to maximize or minimize.

functions.name: nabs.optimize.golden_section_search
documentation:
Use golden-section search to find the extrema of a signal

The algorithm is fed the starting range in which the extrema is contained
within and a tolerance in which we would like to know the position of the
extrema. For the algorithm to succeed it is a requirement that the
underlying distribution is unimodal. After beginning the scan, "probe"
points will be chosen to help determine how to narrow the range which
contains the extrema. These probes are chosen using the golden ratio so the
scan will complete in a deterministic number of iterations based on the
starting range and desired resolution.

# Parameters

signal

: Signal whose distribution we are investigating

motor

: Object that is `set` to probe different points of the distribution.

tolerance

: The size of the range we would like to narrow the position of our
  extrema. Note that this is not the tolerance that we will know the
  "value" of the extrema, but instead the resolution we will be sure that
  it lies within on the "x" axis

limits

: Starting bounds that we know the extrema lie within

average

: Option to average the signal we are reading to limit the affect of
  noise on our measurements

# Returns

bounds

: The range in which we have determined the extrema to lie within.

modules.name: nabs.plans
documentation:
Standalone `bluesky` plans for data collection.

This is the LCLS counterpart to `bluesky.plans`.

All plans in this module will work as-is when passed into a
`bluesky.run_engine.RunEngine`, including starting and stopping a run.

Plans preceded by "daq" incorporate running the LCLS DAQ in the plan.

functions.name: nabs.plans.duration_scan
documentation:
Generalized version of the `delay_scan` movement pattern.

This is a bluesky plan that moves motors among points for a fixed
duration of time, rather than through a finite number of points.

For each motor, a list of points must be provided. Each motor will be moved
through its list of points simultaneously if multiple motors are provided.

This will take a reading at every scan step by default via
`bluesky.plan_stubs.trigger_and_read.`

At the end of the scan, the motors will be returned to their original
positions.

# Parameters

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

args :

: For one dimension, `motor, [point1, point2, ....]`.
  In general:

  ```python
  motor1, [point1, point2, ...],
  motor2, [point1, point2, ...],
  ...,
  motorN, [point1, point2, ...]
  ```

  Motors can be any 'settable' object (motor, temp controller, etc.)

duration

: The time to run in seconds.

per_step

: An alternate plan to run for every scan point. Defaults to
  `bluesky.plan_stubs.one_nd_step`.

md

: Additional metadata to include in the bluesky start document.

functions.name: nabs.plans.delay_scan
documentation:
A `bluesky` plan that sets up and executes the delay scan.

A delay scan is one that moves a
`pcdsdevices.epics_motor.DelayNewport` back and forth.
Underneath, this moves a physical motor that changes the path length
of an optical laser, thus changing the timing of the laser shot by
introducing a delay.

This is a `duration_scan` in one dimension that also sets the stage
velocity to match the configured sweep time and returns the motor to the
starting position at the end of the scan.

See `daq_delay_scan` for the version with DAQ support.

# Parameters

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

time_motor

: The movable device in egu seconds.

time_points

: The times in second to move between.

sweep_time

: The duration we take to move from one end of the range to the other.

duration

: If provided, the time to run in seconds. If omitted, we'll run forever.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_delay_scan
documentation:
Scan a laser delay timing motor with DAQ support.

A delay scan is one that moves a
`pcdsdevices.epics_motor.DelayNewport` back and forth.
Underneath, this moves a physical motor that changes the path length
of an optical laser, thus changing the timing of the laser shot by
introducing a delay.

This is a `duration_scan` in one dimension that also sets the stage
velocity to match the configured sweep time and returns the motor to the
starting position at the end of the scan. It is the `delay_scan` with
the DAQ added.

Unlike the majority of DAQ scans, this scan does not include the delay
stage as a control variable in the DAQ. This is because the "points" of the
scan are not relevant to the data: we only care about the intermediate
shots. In fact, reconfiguring the DAQ with the new controls positions only
slows us down and makes the data more annoying to analyze!

Parameters
----------
detectors : list of readables
    Objects to read into Python in the scan.

time_motor : `pcdsdevices.epics_motor.DelayNewport`
    The movable device in egu seconds.

time_points : list of float
    The times in second to move between.

sweep_time : float
    The duration we take to move from one end of the range to the other.

duration : float, optional
    If provided, the time to run in seconds. If omitted, we'll run forever.

record : bool, optional
    Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
    we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_count
documentation:
Take repeated DAQ runs with no motors.

This is an LCLS-I DAQ version of `bluesky`'s built-in
`bluesky.plans.count` plan.

# Parameters

detectors

: List of 'readable' objects to read at every step.

num

: Number of readings to take; default is 1.
  If None, capture data until canceled.

delay

: Time delay in seconds between successive readings; default is 0.

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

per_shot

: Hook for customizing action of inner loop (messages per step).
  Expected signature

  ```
  def f(detectors: Iterable[OphydObj]) -> Generator[Msg]:
      ...
  ```

  See docstring of `bluesky.plan_stubs.one_shot` (the default)
  for details.

md

: Additional metadata to include in the start document.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.
The rest of this file contains thin wrappers around bluesky built-ins.
These exist to mimic an older hutch python API,
easing the transition to bluesky.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_scan
documentation:
Scan through a multi-motor (start, end, num) trajectory with DAQ support.

This is an LCLS-I DAQ version of `bluesky`'s built-in
`bluesky.plans.scan` plan. It also returns the motors to their starting
points after the scan is complete.

# Parameters

detectors

: List of 'readable' objects to read at every step.

args :

: For one dimension, `motor, start, stop`.
  In general:

  ```python
  motor1, start1, stop1,
  motor2, start2, start2,
  ...,
  motorN, startN, stopN
  ```

  Motors can be any 'settable' object (motor, temp controller, etc.)

num

: Number of points.

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

per_step

: Hook for customizing action of inner loop (messages per step).
  See docstring of `bluesky.plan_stubs.one_nd_step` (the default)
  for details.

md

: Additional metadata to include in the start document.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_list_scan
documentation:
Scan through a multi-motor list trajectory with DAQ support.

This is an LCLS-I DAQ version of `bluesky`'s built-in
`bluesky.plans.list_scan` plan. It also returns the motors
to their starting points after the scan is complete.

# Parameters

detectors

: List of 'readable' objects to read at every step.

args :

: For one dimension, `motor, [point1, point2, ....]`.
  In general:

  ```python
  motor1, [point1, point2, ...],
  motor2, [point1, point2, ...],
  ...,
  motorN, [point1, point2, ...]
  ```

  Motors can be any 'settable' object (motor, temp controller, etc.)

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

per_step

: Hook for customizing action of inner loop (messages per step).
  See docstring of `bluesky.plan_stubs.one_nd_step` (the default)
  for details.

md

: Additional metadata to include in the start document.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_ascan
documentation:
One-dimensional daq scan with absolute positions.

This moves a motor from start to end in nsteps steps, taking data in the
DAQ at every step, and returning the motor to its original position at
the end of the scan.

# Parameters

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

motor

: A movable object to scan.

start

: The first point in the scan.

end

: The last point in the scan.

n

: if int, the number of points in the scan.
  if float, step size

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_dscan
documentation:
One-dimensional daq scan with relative (delta) positions.

This moves a motor from current_pos + start to current_pos + end
in nsteps steps, taking data in the DAQ at every step, and
returning the motor to its original position at the end of the scan.

# Parameters

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

motor

: A movable object to scan.

start

: The first point in the scan, relative to the current position.

end

: The last point in the scan, relative to the current position.

n

: if int, the number of points in the scan.
  if float, step size

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.

This moves two motors from start to end in nsteps steps, taking data in

b1

: The last point in the scan for m1.

m2

: The second movable object to scan.

: The first point in the scan for m2.

b2

: The last point in the scan for m2.

nsteps

: The number of points in the scan.

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because

use_l3t

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_d2scan
documentation:
Two-dimensional daq scan with relative positions

This moves two motors from start to end relative to their current positions
in nsteps steps, taking data in the DAQ at every step, and returning the

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

m1

: The first movable object to scan.

a1

b1

: The last point in the scan for m1, relative to the current position.

m2

: The second movable object to scan.

a2

: The first point in the scan for m2, relative to the current position.

b2

: The last point in the scan for m2, relative to the current position.

nsteps

: The number of points in the scan.

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_anscan
documentation:
N-dimensional daq scan with absolute positions.

This moves N motors from start to end in nsteps steps, taking data in
the DAQ at every step, and returning the motors to their original positions
at the end of the scan.

Takes an arbitrary number of motors, start and stop positions

>> RE(daq_anscan([], m1, a1, b1,

: m2, a2, b2, ... ,
  mn, an, bn, nsteps,
  events=10))

# Parameters

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

m1

: The first movable object to scan.

a1

: The first point in the scan for m1.

b1

: The last point in the scan for m1.

nsteps

: The number of points in the scan.

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.

duration

: Duration of time to spend at each step. If omitted, uses the events
  argument or the last configured value.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

use_l3t

: Whether or not the use the l3t filter for the events argument. Defaults
  to False to avoid confusion from unconfigured filters.

# Note

The events, duration, record, and use_l3t arguments come from the
{py:func}`nabs.preprocessors.daq_step_scan_decorator`.

documentation:
N-dimensional daq scan with relative positions.

This moves N motors from start to end in nsteps steps, taking data in
the DAQ at every step, and returning the motors to their original positions

>> RE(daq_dnscan([], m1, a1, b1,

: m2, a2, b2, ... ,
  mn, an, bn, nsteps,
  events=10))

# Parameters

detectors

m1

a1

b1

duration

record

functions.name: nabs.plans.fixed_target_scan
documentation:
Scan over two variables in steps simultaneously.

This is a `nabs` version of `bluesky`'s built-in
`bluesky.plans.list_scan` plan.
This scan is designed to be used with 3 motors, the x and y motors that
move to a designaget target on a sample wafer, and one scan_motor that can
be delay time, laser power or some other motor positions.

# Parameters

sample

: The name of the sample we're interested in.

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

x_motor

: Motor object corresponding to the x axes.

y_motor

: Motor object corresponding to the y axes.

scan_motor

: The motor being scanned. It can be e.g., delay time, laser power, some
  other motor position, etc.

ss

: List of all the points (samples) for the scan_motor to go through.

n_shots

: Indicates how many shots should be taken, or how many samples should
  be scanned on the grid.

path

: Path where the sample file is located.

functions.name: nabs.plans.fixed_target_multi_scan
documentation:
Scan over three variables in steps simultaneously.

This function allows for multiple shots at the same target.

This is a `nabs` version of `bluesky`'s built-in
`bluesky.plans.list_scan` plan.
This scan is designed to be used with 3 motors, the x and y motors that
move to a designaget target on a sample wafer, and one scan_motor that can
be delay time, laser power or some other motor positions.

# Parameters

sample

: The name of the sample we're interested in.

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

x_motor

: Motor object corresponding to the x axes.

y_motor

: Motor object corresponding to the y axes.

scan_motor

: The motor being scanned. It can be e.g., delay time, laser power, some
  other motor position, etc.

ss

: List of all the points (samples) for the scan_motor to go through.

n_shots

: Indicates how many shots should be taken at one position.

path

: Path where the sample file is located.

functions.name: nabs.plans.daq_fixed_target_scan
documentation:
Scan over two variables in steps simultaneously with DAQ Support.

This is a `nabs` version of `bluesky`'s built-in
`bluesky.plans.list_scan` plan. It takes in consideration the number of
samples to be scanned out of the samples given in `xx` and `yy`.

# Parameters

sample

: The name of the sample we're interested in.

detectors

: Objects to read into Python in the scan.

x_motor

: Motor object corresponding to the x axes.

y_motor

: Motor object corresponding to the y axes.

scan_motor

: The motor being scanned. It can be e.g., delay time, laser power, some
  other motor position, etc.

ss

: List of all the points (samples) for the scan_motor to go through.

n_shots

: Indicates how many shots should be taken, or how many samples should
  be scanned on the grid.

path

: Path where the sample file is located.

record

: Whether or not to record the run in the DAQ. Defaults to True because
  we don't want to accidentally skip recording good runs.

events

: Number of events to take at each step. If omitted, uses the
  duration argument or the last configured value.
